subject: SIKORSKY S76C++ (S/N 760667)
body:
FYI see below, Darren how would you like to proceed if JEE is  interensted?
LV



**************Planning your summer road trip? Check out AOL Travel Guides.    
  (http://travel.aol.com/travel-guide/united-states?ncid=aoltrv00030000000016)


From: "Sean J. Lancaster" <slancaster@bristolassociates.com>
Subject: FW: SIKORSKY S76C++ (S/N 760667)
Date: Tue, 08 Apr 2008 14:02:08 GMT
To: lvjet@aol.com

Dear Mr. Epstein, 

 

Please find a letter from a Gentleman based in Sunrise Florida who
represents himself as a parts distributor of Helicopter parts for Mexico,
Central and South America.   He claims to have a customer by the name of
Francisco J. Gonzales {undisclosed location} that will pay $13.3-13.5 for
the Helicopter immediately but Mr. Moreau wants the monies above $13.0.  I
have told Mr. Moreau to send a formal Letter of intent on several occasions
and have even offered a format for him.  I also have told him we need full
disclosure about the actual buyer and his funding sources.

 

I cannot qualify this deal at this point without taking this to the next
steps wherein we respond to this with a formal agreement.  Larry brings up a
good point that selling a higher price and paying a fee to the broker may
cause a tax consequence.  In any event if this is of any interest can we
have Darren speak with Mr. Moreau about this deal and a structure that
protects your interests?  

 

 

 

Best regards 

 

 

Sean J. Lancaster

Bristol Associates Inc.

1-540-665-5135 direct

1-202-682-4000 ext 1130 Main Office

1-540-533-8183 Mobile

1-540-301-0050 FAX

slancaster@bristolassociates.com

www.bristolassociates.com <http://www.bristolassociates.com/>  

 

This message may contain confidential and/or legally privileged information.
If you are not the intended recipient of the message, please destroy it. Any
unauthorized dissemination, distribution or copying of the material in this
message, and any attachments to the message, is strictly forbidden. (c) 2008
all rights reserved.

 

 

  _____  

From: corpomerica@aol.com [mailto:corpomerica@aol.com] 
Sent: Monday, April 07, 2008 6:12 PM
To: slancaster@bristolassociates.com
Subject: Re: SIKORSKY S76C++ (S/N 760667)

 

Dear Sean,

I will need a Confidential Sale Agreement in the form of a notarized letter
from his attorney or from your company stating that the ownwer will pay back
to CORPOMERICA, INC. any excess received over $13,0MM from the sale of the
helicopter in reference to my customer Francisco J. Gonzalez. The sale will
be paid in full by a wire transfer from europe, from a prime Bank. 

The asking price should be $13,5MM but not less than $13,3MM. Once we have
received the Confidential Sale Agreement we can make arrangements for an
appoiment to check the helicopter in Palm Beach.

Best Regards,

Jose Moreau
CORPOMERICA, INC.
1304 SW 160 Avenue Ste 202
Sunrise, Florida 33326
Ph (954) 389-5758 Fax (954) 389-8987
Mob (954) 326-6692

Dear Mr. Epstein,

 

Please find a letter from a Gentleman based in Sunrise Florida who represents himself as a parts distributor of Helicopter parts for Mexico, Central and South America.   He claims to have a customer by the name of Francisco J. Gonzales {undisclosed location} that will pay $13.3-13.5 for the Helicopter immediately but Mr. Moreau wants the monies above $13.0.  I have told Mr. Moreau to send a formal Letter of intent on several occasions and have even offered a format for him.  I also have told him we need full disclosure about the actual buyer and his funding sources.

 

I cannot qualify this deal at this point without taking this to the next steps wherein we respond to this with a formal agreement.  Larry brings up a good point that selling a higher price and paying a fee to the broker may cause a tax consequence.  In any event if this is of any interest can we have Darren speak with Mr. Moreau about this deal and a structure that protects your interests?
